metaphors.meaning: Keeper of surfaces: the building belongs to him, and no one sees him
The buried: sent behind the sealed door, his cleanliness was a cover
The mirror: he was the mechanism, and he stops cleaning

metaphors.manifestation: He ran the buffer until the corridor came up shining and his own face moved in the floor beneath him.
metaphors.meaning: Pride in a surface no one notices
The choice: carry it away once more, or set it down

metaphors.manifestation: His supervisor put a hand on his shoulder, mentioned a raise, and said not to worry about B-wing. Maintenance had that one covered now.
metaphors.meaning: The institution begins to clean him

metaphors.manifestation: Two weeks later they collected his keys at the gate.
metaphors.meaning: Cost: to refuse to carry refuse is to become it

metaphors.manifestation: He held the door. Forty-one of them went through. The forty-second said thanks to the door.
metaphors.meaning: He is looked through

metaphors.manifestation: The drain backed up. He reached past the trap and brought out a student ID, swollen, the photo still readable. The name under it was Mara Okafor.
metaphors.meaning: The erased name surfaces

metaphors.manifestation: Behind the new drywall the old doorframe was still there, painted over. The floor inside was a fresh sheet of grey.
metaphors.meaning: A room hidden inside a renovation

metaphors.manifestation: At six the last of them filed out and the building became his.
metaphors.meaning: He has the building once the seen people leave

metaphors.manifestation: He never buffed that corridor again. The floor went dull, and his face stopped coming up in it.
metaphors.meaning: Payoff: he declines to disappear into the surface

metaphors.manifestation: On his way out he left the wing's lights burning.
metaphors.meaning: He lets the truth stay visible

metaphors.manifestation: Pell. Vance. Holloway, cut into the stone over each door. His own name was a strip of masking tape on locker 12, re-inked where it had worn.
metaphors.meaning: The names that count and the name that does not

metaphors.manifestation: He knew the room by the way the mop had dragged. He had worked it once, on overtime, signed for the hours, and never asked whose night it had been.
metaphors.meaning: Complicity: he was here before

metaphors.manifestation: His ring opened every door in the building. One it never opened: the wing they had posted for asbestos eight years ago and never touched.
metaphors.meaning: Every door but one

metaphors.manifestation: He had painted the floor three times. Each morning the dark came back up through the newest grey, a little east of where he had centered it.
metaphors.meaning: The thing he is paid to make disappear

metaphors.manifestation: He set the roller down in the tray and left the floor the way it was.
metaphors.meaning: He stops cleaning

metaphors.manifestation: He taped Mara Okafor's ID to locker 12, beside the strip with his own name. The only two names in the building that had not been paid for.
metaphors.meaning: Payoff: he files her name where his own is kept

metaphors.manifestation: The work order said PREP B-WING FOR DEMO. Clipped beneath it was a key he had never been issued.
metaphors.meaning: Inciting: he is sent in
What is sealed away and discarded
Truth is visible only on the night shift
What will not come out
Donor names in stone; his own in masking tape
The keys of the invisible man
The student ID with the erased name
The floor you can see your face in
Names carved, taped, or erased
The one door his ring will not open
The dark that comes back through the paint
An institution stays clean only because someone is paid to carry away what it refuses to admit
Looked through; the keys of the invisible
